title: Copy, Replace, Rename, Delete, Batch, and Concurrency · Stakeholder Go Mission
summary: A multi-tenant case-management provider. They need a streaming Go gateway, strict tenant authorization, conditional ranges and writes, database and object reconciliation, recoverable rename and delete, duplicate-safe events, and orphan repair. Planned artifact: Deliver Tenant-Safe File API and Metadata Reconciler with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging…
objectives: Apply trusted authority, authentication, authorization, method, route, headers, body, multipart, rate, and concurrency admission before storage work.
Validate one bounded range, map inclusive offsets, cap response bytes, preserve Content-Range, and reject unsatisfiable or abusive requests.
Persist idempotent intent, reservation, object identity, expected integrity, upload, quarantine, publish, failure, and expiry transitions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Commit to the mission model · Project Http Request Admission
steps.instruction:
project case — A project Go file-delivery team handles case 4966 during a high-traffic evidence intake window. Build deterministic Go evidence to model a non-atomic version-aware copy, validate, and delete rename protocol; do not treat an ETag, CORS result, emulator, or successful SDK call as stronger proof than it is; then introduce a duplicate event or ambiguous response and reconcile without duplicate effect. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and Go Commit to the mission model · Project Http Request Admission.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for apply trusted authority, authentication, authorization, method, route, headers, body, multipart, rate, and concurrency admission before storage work.
